summary: Web project with navbar/header work in progress. Logo asset loading broken in production (broken image icon in navbar).
known_issues: Logo image not loading in production navbar — broken image icon appears; likely asset path, case-sensitivity, or build inclusion issue.

existing_features.title: Audit the logo image reference in `frontend/src/components/Header.tsx`: verify the `src` attribute path exactly matches the actual filename on disk (including case sensitivity), confirm the asset exists under `frontend/public/` or the correct static assets directory, and ensure the path used is compatible with the production build output (e.g., using a root-relative `/` prefix or correct Vite asset import rather than a relative path that breaks in nested routes).

Also verify and fix the logo image reference in `frontend/src/components/Footer.tsx` using the same corrected path/import approach applied to the Header, so the logo is consistent and working across the entire site.

new_feature_requests.summary: Ensure the logo asset file is physically present in `frontend/public/` (or the appropriate static assets folder) and is included in the production build output. If the file is missing, add or restore it. If it was previously imported via a JS/TS import rather than placed in `public/`, standardise the approach so it is reliably bundled.
